title: Coelogyne atjehensis
intro: Coelogyne atjehensis is a species of orchid native to Indonesia, characterized by its unique, terrestrial growth habit and showy white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne atjehensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, although further research is needed to fully understand its safety and efficacy.
taxonomy: Coelogyne atjehensis is a species of orchid in the genus Coelogyne. This genus is a part of the subtribe Coelogyneinae within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is a part of the family Orchidaceae, which is commonly known as the orchid family. The family Orchidaceae is further divided into seven subfamilies, with Epidendroideae being one of the largest and most diverse. Within the family Orchidaceae, the subfamily Epidendroideae is characterized by a range of growth habits, from terrestrial to epiphytic and lithophytic forms.
